raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the response focus primarily on high-level research/methodological critique or on prose-level writing polish?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request could reasonably ask whether the section frames the protocol, contribution, and takeaway effectively.","R1.2":"Assessing whether the protocol description and inferential chain cohere is a central likely responsibility for general feedback.","R1.3":"The artifact reports accuracy ranges and a correlation, so checking whether the stated conclusions are supported by those reported results is centrally implicated.","R1.6":"Because the artifact is a draft passage, prose quality, repetition, flow, and concision are central likely targets of the delegation.","RX.1":"Any projection must acknowledge uncertainty because the delegation is highly underspecified.","RX.2":"The response must avoid inflating what can be inferred from the reported results, especially around correlation and self-recognition claims.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projection rather than actually giving feedback or revising the artifact.","RX.4":"The response should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported evaluator conclusions downstream.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the response focus primarily on high-level research/methodological critique or on prose-level writing polish?

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request could reasonably ask whether the section frames the protocol, contribution, and takeaway effectively.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing whether the protocol description and inferential chain cohere is a central likely responsibility for general feedback.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact reports accuracy ranges and a correlation, so checking whether the stated conclusions are supported by those reported results is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.6: Because the artifact is a draft passage, prose quality, repetition, flow, and concision are central likely targets of the delegation.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must acknowledge uncertainty because the delegation is highly underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid inflating what can be inferred from the reported results, especially around correlation and self-recognition claims.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection rather than actually giving feedback or revising the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The response should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported evaluator conclusions downstream.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
